---
title: "Learning Objectives"
format: html
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
```

# Practical 1 - Using R and RStudio

::: {.callout-tip .objectives}
## Learning objectives

### @sec-p1_setup

#### Sections
- @sec-git_github
- @sec-setup_rstudio

By the end of this part of the practical you should be able to:

- Explain what Git and GitHub are
- Create a GitHub account and fork a repository
- Clone your repository into RStudio

### @sec-p2_r_and_rstudio

#### Sections
- @sec-r_language
- @sec-coding_in_the_console
- @sec-rstudio_interface
- @sec-console_scripts_quarto
- @sec-documenting_and_formatting_your_code

By the end of this part of the practical you should be able to:

- Explain what R is and why it is useful for biological data analysis.
- Use some features of the RStudio interface
- Use the R console, scripts and Quarto documents to run R code.
- Create a simple reproducible analysis using Quarto.
- Understand the benefits of writing clear, well-formatted and documented R code.

### @sec-p3_functions_and_variables

- Functions
- Variables and variable names
- Types of variable

##### [Part 4: Vectors, data frames and plots](01-04_complex_variables_plotting.qmd)

- Multi-Element Variables
- Vectors
- Indices
- Data frames
- Introduction to plotting in R

##### Part 5 - Extension Exercises
:::
